fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.considerations: Multiple Access Points; Limited Rear Access
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access to the property may be achieved by ensuring that all access points are clearly marked and easily identifiable from major approach routes. Improving visibility of these access points could facilitate quicker entry during emergencies. Addressing the limited rear access by creating or designating additional pathways may allow for better maneuverability and access to all sides of the property. Adequate signage and well-maintained pedestrian walkways would aid in clearly directing responders to key areas, thereby supporting effective response operations. Additionally, ensuring that vehicle parking areas are organized to prevent obstruction of vital paths could assist in the staging and movement of emergency vehicles, enhancing overall response efficacy.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Tree Cluster; Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Open Terrain
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property by increasing the separation between vegetation and structures could reduce potential wildfire exposure. Pruning or thinning the scattered shrubs and trees in proximity to the residence may help to reduce fuel concentrations and support the integrity of open terrain buffers. Maintaining open terrains and minimizing vegetation density around the perimeter can break fuel continuity, potentially limiting the spread of fire toward the structure. Managing the tree cluster by selectively thinning dense areas and removing lower branches may improve fuel separation and reduce ladder fuels. Regular maintenance of the vegetated perimeter is crucial for long-term wildfire resilience, supporting both suppression efforts and firefighter access. Such measures, combined with the management of vegetation adjacent to the structure, could enhance property preparedness and contribute to wildfire mitigation.
